title: GPTQ + Short TTT — val_bpb 1.1207 (seed 1337)
body:
## Summary

- **val_bpb: 1.1207** (single seed 1337)
- **Artifact: 15.60 MB** (int6+zstd-22)
- **Training: 600s** on 8xH100 SXM | **Eval: 268s** (75s sliding + 192s TTT)

Iterating on our previous submission (#508, 1.1215 three-seed mean). Same base architecture and GPTQ pipeline, with one change to the TTT strategy. Marginal improvement — sharing findings for the community.

## What Changed from #508

### Short TTT (no EMA, 50 chunks)

Our #508 used EMA-smoothed TTT over 200 chunks (decay=0.995). We observed that TTT contributed +0.0000 to the final score — EMA was smoothing away all adaptation gains.

We tried a more aggressive approach: SGD TTT with no EMA, stopping training after 50 chunks. The running average peaks at chunk 51 (bpb=1.1104) but degrades through chunks 100–400 before recovering. Final TTT score: 1.12075 — essentially matching the pre-TTT sliding window of 1.12070.

**Conclusion**: short TTT avoids the late-chunk degradation of full TTT, but the adapted weights don't generalize well to the middle portion of the validation set. TTT is net neutral on this architecture.

### Other Fixes

- Proper zstd-22 compression (shaved ~2MB vs zlib fallback in an earlier broken run)
- Disabled int8_sensitive to stay within 16MB

## Results

| Metric | #508 (seed 1337) | This run |
|--------|------------------|----------|
| Pre-quant (post-EMA) | 1.1386 | 1.1387 |
| Post-quant roundtrip | 1.1444 | 1.1445 |
| Sliding window (s64) | 1.1206 | **1.1207** |
| Legal TTT | 1.1206 | **1.1208** |
| Artifact | 15.56 MB | 15.60 MB |

Effectively the same base model — variance is within seed noise.

## TTT Chunk Trajectory

The short TTT (50 chunks, no EMA) shows clear adaptation then distribution shift:

| Chunk range | Running BPB | Interpretation |
|-------------|------------|----------------|
| 1–50 | 1.158→1.110 | Adapting (training active) |
| 51 (peak) | **1.1104** | Training stops, weights frozen |
| 100–200 | 1.119→1.137 | Distribution shift — adapted weights hurt |
| 400–900 | 1.138→1.127 | Gradual recovery |
| 1300–1893 | 1.125→1.123 | Convergence to final |

The chunk-51 peak (1.1104) demonstrates the model has capacity for much lower BPB. The challenge is making that adaptation generalize across the full validation set.

## Architecture

Unchanged from #508: 11L/512d/8H/4KV/3xMLP (relu²), U-Net skip, Partial RoPE (16/64), XSA last 4, BigramHash(2048), VE128 on layers 9-10, SmearGate. 26.99M params.

## Credits

- Base architecture: @signalrush (PR #414)
- Legal TTT recipe: @anantdgoel (PR #461)
- GPTQ: Frantar et al. (2022)

## Run

```bash
TTT_OPTIMIZER=sgd TTT_LR=0.002 TTT_EPOCHS=3 TTT_FREEZE_BLOCKS=2 \
TTT_EMA_DECAY=0 TTT_MAX_TRAIN_CHUNKS=50 INT8_SENSITIVE="" \
SEED=1337 torchrun --standalone --nproc_per_node=8 train_gpt_v7.py
```

Requires Flash Attention 3 (Hopper selective build).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
